title: ACDP Error Response
description: Standard error response returned by ACDP endpoints on failure.

code.description: A machine-readable error code from the ACDP standard error vocabulary. invalid_receipt is 0.2.0+ (RFC-ACDP-0010 §8, §11); immutable_field (activated from the v0.1.0 reservation, RFC-ACDP-0009 §2.1) and invalid_lifecycle_transition are 0.3.0+ lifecycle codes (RFC-ACDP-0013 §6, §10); invalid_log_proof is a 0.3.0+ transparency-log code (RFC-ACDP-0012 §9, §11); invalid_witness_cosignature is a 0.4.0+ witness-cosigning code (RFC-ACDP-0015 §8, §10) — deliberately distinct from invalid_log_proof (it indicts a witness's attestation, not the registry's log); none of these may be emitted by registries declaring an earlier acdp_version. Codes reserved for future versions (not in this enum): unsupported_embedding_model (a future version's similarity endpoints).

details.description: Optional structured details about the error. Shape is error-code-specific. Recognized keys per code: superseded_target → 'reason' (one of 'not_found', 'lineage_mismatch', 'version_mismatch', 'already_superseded', 'cross_registry_supersession_unsupported'); invalid_signature → 'key_id', 'algorithm'; key_resolution_failed / key_resolution_unreachable → 'key_id', 'underlying' (transport-layer message); duplicate_publish → 'idempotency_key', 'original_ctx_id'. Registries MAY include additional keys; consumers MUST tolerate unknown keys.
